name: Beijing
lifestyle: Political and cultural capital with expat families clustered in Shunyi near international schools. Winters are dry with occasional air-quality episodes, and premium housing plus tuition dominate the top-tier budget. Z visa sponsorship is the standard entry path.

true_fat_fire.description: Sanlitun/CBD high-floor apartment or Shunyi villa, full-time ayi plus driver, fine dining weekly, air-purifier suite, 3+ international escapes annually.

guide.groceries: Imported-food baskets, embassy-area premium grocers, and high-quality fruit, meat, and wine at home without much budget pressure.
guide.dining: Embassy-zone or hotel dining several times a week, duck or banquet meals regularly, with nicer nights often RMB800-1,800 per person.
guide.rhythm: Driver support, ayi coverage, premium indoor wellness, and a travel pattern that treats air-quality escapes and long weekends as routine.

comfortable_expat.description: Chaoyang/Dongcheng 2BR hutong-adjacent or mid-rise, dining out 3-4 nights/week, part-time ayi, weekend trips plus annual international.

guide.groceries: A mix of Hema, Jenny Lou's-style imported shelves, and local produce or meat restocks for a comfortable expat kitchen.
guide.dining: Restaurants a few nights a week plus the occasional nicer dinner, often RMB120-240 per person casually and RMB500-1,000 on a bigger night.
guide.rhythm: Subway plus Didi, moderate wellness and grooming, periodic housekeeping help, and domestic or regional breaks rather than nonstop luxury travel.

luxury_family.description: Shunyi villa compound near WAB/ISB, full-time ayi plus driver, top international school access when dependents apply, frequent air-quality escapes plus annual home-country trip.

guide.groceries: Large family provisioning through imported grocers, warehouse-style top-ups, and bulk delivery with school, snack, and home-entertaining needs built in.
guide.dining: Family compound or hotel dining most weekends plus adult meals out, with casual family meals often RMB150-300 per person and special dinners well above that.
guide.rhythm: Ayi and driver support, premium school logistics, indoor sports or tutoring, and regular escape travel timed around school breaks and weather.
